top_signals.title: Venezuela’s oil-linked debt with China could complicate its restructuring push
top_signals.summary: Venezuela’s plan to restructure its sovereign and oil company debt could boost oil investor confidence, but the country’s debt to China could be a hurdle to the final resolution of the matter, experts say. top_signals.title: FSRUs gaining strategic importance in a changing LNG landscape
top_signals.summary: Once deployed primarily in response to short-term supply disruptions, FSRUs are increasingly being repositioned as long-term infrastructure. As LNG markets expand and energy systems evolve, their role is being reshaped by the need for flexibility, faster deployment, and reduced investment risk. The role of floating storage and regasification units (FSRUs) is shifting rapidly. Traditionally viewed ...

top_signals.title: VLCC Orders Drive Tanker Newbuilding Surge
top_signals.summary: Tanker newbuild ordering has gathered significant momentum through 2026, increasing c.358% year-on-year with 444 vessels placed year to date (including on order and options) across all major segments. Activity peaked in February at 20.5 mil DWT before moderating in subsequent months. Ordering has skewed heavily towards large crude carriers, with VLCCs and Suezmax vessels commanding ...

top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index Falls to Over 6-Week Low
top_signals.summary: The Baltic Exchange dry bulk freight index decreased 0.3% to 2720 on Monday, the lowest since April 30. The capesize index, which typically transport 150,000-ton cargoes including iron ore and coal, fell 1.3% to 4053. On the other hand, the panamax index, which usually carry 60,000 to 70,000 tons of coal or grain, increased 0.4% ...

alerts.title: Hormuz Traffic Near Zero as Dark Exports Continue
alerts.summary: The Strait of Hormuz remained under acute operational pressure between June 11 and June 14. AIS-visible commercial traffic stayed near zero, IRGC small-craft activity surged, and covert export infrastructure continued functioning despite sustained U.S. enforcement. The most immediate signal is the collapse in visible movement. Only one AIS crossing was detected from June 11 to ...
